title: Gyrotheca
taxonomy: Gyrotheca belongs to the family Bignoniaceae, commonly known as the Bignonia or trumpet vine family. This family consists of over 1,000 species of flowering plants, many of which are vines or shrubs. Gyrotheca is a genus of climbing plants, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plants in this genus are known for their showy, trumpet-shaped flowers and woody stems. They are epiphytes, meaning they grow on other plants or surfaces, such as trees or rocks, without deriving nutrients from them.
